title: Autonomous Drone Racing with Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yunlong Song; Mats Steinweg; Elia Kaufmann; Davide Scaramuzza
venue: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
discovery_query: "drone racing" randomized tracks gate sequence reinforcement learning
representation_family: ordered 6-DoF gate poses; relative gate primitives
generation_role: generates random training tracks and unseen validation/test tracks
validity_strategy: bounded relative gate position and orientation
geometry_metrics: gate count; track length 110-150 m; elevation change up to 17 m; safety margin
difficulty_metrics: curriculum stage; crash count; success rate; lap time
diversity_metrics: 100 unseen validation tracks; 1000 random test tracks; three difficulty levels
training_distribution: 100 parallel configurations per iteration; random relative gate transforms; 5/15/30/35-gate curricula reported
evaluation_suite: Split-S 7 gates; AlphaPilot 11; AirSim 21; 1000 random configurations; 97.4% random-track success
reproducibility_fields: relative-pose sampling bounds; parallel environment count; gate counts; test counts; track extents
evidence_locator: https://rpg.ifi.uzh.ch/docs/IROS21_Yunlong.pdf, Secs. IV-C and V-C, Fig. 3, Tables I-II; https://github.com/uzh-rpg/flightmare, README and assets
coding_notes: Geometry generator is distinct from trajectory optimization and dynamics randomization. Paper-specific generator-code availability was not established from a row-specific official availability surface; public Flightmare code and assets are dependency resources. Explicit global self-intersection checking was not reported.
